edges.description: Disrupted CIITA/RFX machinery prevents normal MHC-II gene transcription.
Failed HLA class II transcription leads to absent or reduced surface MHC-II expression.
edges.target: MHC-II expression and antigen presentation failure

edges.description: Loss of MHC-II antigen presentation disrupts CD4 T-cell development and helper activity.
edges.source: MHC-II expression and antigen presentation failure
edges.target: CD4 T-cell selection and helper-function impairment

edges.description: Absence of MHC-II expression in thymic epithelium perturbs central tolerance.
edges.source: MHC-II expression and antigen presentation failure

edges.description: CD4 helper-cell impairment disrupts both cellular and humoral immune responses.
edges.source: CD4 T-cell selection and helper-function impairment
edges.target: Combined cellular and humoral immune deficiency

edges.description: Defective cellular immunity manifests as impaired T-cell function.
edges.source: Combined cellular and humoral immune deficiency

edges.description: Defective T-cell help reduces antibody production.
edges.source: Combined cellular and humoral immune deficiency

edges.description: Combined immune deficiency predisposes to severe recurrent respiratory infections.
edges.source: Combined cellular and humoral immune deficiency

edges.description: Combined immune deficiency predisposes to recurrent gastrointestinal infection and diarrhea.
edges.source: Combined cellular and humoral immune deficiency

edges.description: Central and peripheral tolerance perturbation contributes to autoimmune manifestations.

edges.target: MHC-II expression and antigen presentation failure

edges.source: Antibiotic prophylaxis and nutritional supportive care
edges.target: Combined cellular and humoral immune deficiency

nodes.description: Infection prophylaxis and nutritional support are used to reduce infectious morbidity and maintain growth while definitive therapy is pursued.
nodes.id: Antibiotic prophylaxis and nutritional supportive care

evidence.reference_title: Hematopoietic stem cell transplantation and other management strategies for MHC class II deficiency.
evidence.snippet: The optimal symptomatic care that is available involves the prophylactic use of antibiotics and the administration of immunoglobulin with adequate nutritional support.

nodes.description: Autoimmune manifestations occur in a subset of patients and may reflect central and peripheral tolerance perturbation.

evidence.reference_title: Immunodeficiency by defective expression of MHC class II (Orphanet structured-database record)
evidence.snippet: HP:0002960 | Autoimmunity | Occasional (29-5%)

nodes.description: MHC-II deficiency impairs thymic CD4-positive T-cell development and peripheral helper T-cell activation, reducing cellular immunity and T-cell-dependent antibody production.
nodes.id: CD4 T-cell selection and helper-function impairment

evidence.reference_title: Clinical, Immunological, and Molecular Findings in Five Patients with Major Histocompatibility Complex Class II Deficiency from India.
evidence.snippet: The development and thymic shaping of CD4+ T helper (Th) cells require MHC class II-mediated antigen presentation to the T cell receptor (TCR) of CD4 cells.

evidence.reference_title: Immunodeficiency by defective expression of MHC class II (Orphanet structured-database record)
evidence.snippet: CIITA | class II major histocompatibility complex transactivator | hgnc:7067 | Disease-causing germline mutation(s) in

evidence.reference_title: Complementation cloning of an MHC class II transactivator mutated in hereditary MHC class II deficiency (or bare lymphocyte syndrome).
evidence.snippet: Hence, the CIITA gene is essential for MHC class II gene expression and has been shown to be responsible for hereditary MHC class II deficiency.

evidence.reference_title: CIITA / MHC class II deficiency (Definitive)
evidence.snippet: CIITA | HGNC:7067 | MHC class II deficiency | MONDO:0008855 | AR | Definitive

nodes.description: Defective CD4 T-cell help causes combined immunodeficiency, with poor cellular responses and reduced T-cell-dependent antibody production.
nodes.id: Combined cellular and humoral immune deficiency

evidence.reference_title: Hematopoietic stem cell transplantation and other management strategies for MHC class II deficiency.
evidence.snippet: The defect of MHC class II leads to combined immunodeficiency with defective CD4(+) T-cell development and a lack of T helper cell-dependent antibody production by B cells.

nodes.description: Impaired T-cell help reduces T-cell-dependent antibody production, producing decreased circulating immunoglobulin levels in many patients.

evidence.reference_title: Immunodeficiency by defective expression of MHC class II (Orphanet structured-database record)
evidence.snippet: HP:0004313 | Decreased circulating antibody level | Frequent (79-30%)

evidence.reference_title: Clinical, Immunological, and Molecular Findings in Five Patients with Major Histocompatibility Complex Class II Deficiency from India.
evidence.snippet: Serum immunoglobulin levels measured in three patients (P3, P4, and P5) revealed reduced concentrations of IgG, IgA, and IgM consistent with lack of helper T cell-dependent antibody production.

nodes.description: Allogeneic hematopoietic cell transplantation is the only established curative therapy and can restore hematopoietic immune function, although outcomes depend on transplant strategy, donor type, infection burden, and residual nonhematopoietic thymic epithelial defects.

evidence.reference_title: Improved transplant survival and long-term disease outcome in children with MHC class II deficiency.
evidence.snippet: Hematopoietic cell transplantation (HCT) remains the only curative treatment for this condition, but transplant survival in the previously published result was poor.

evidence.reference_title: Improved transplant survival and long-term disease outcome in children with MHC class II deficiency.
evidence.snippet: With a 2.9-year median follow-up, OS improved from 33% (46-68%) for HCT before 2008 (n = 6) to 94% (66-99%) for HCT after 2008

nodes.description: Immunoglobulin replacement is used as supportive management for reduced antibody production while awaiting transplant or when definitive therapy is not immediately available.

evidence.reference_title: Hematopoietic stem cell transplantation and other management strategies for MHC class II deficiency.
evidence.snippet: The optimal symptomatic care that is available involves the prophylactic use of antibiotics and the administration of immunoglobulin with adequate nutritional support.

nodes.description: Impaired T-cell function reflects defective CD4 T-cell development and antigen-driven activation.

evidence.reference_title: Immunodeficiency by defective expression of MHC class II (Orphanet structured-database record)
evidence.snippet: HP:0005354 | Lack of T cell function | Very frequent (99-80%)

nodes.description: HLA-DR, -DP, and -DQ expression is absent or severely reduced on B cells, monocytes, dendritic cells, and thymic epithelial cells, impairing MHC-II antigen presentation.
nodes.id: MHC-II expression and antigen presentation failure

biological_processes: antigen processing and presentation of peptide antigen via MHC class II

evidence.reference_title: Clinical, Immunological, and Molecular Findings in Five Patients with Major Histocompatibility Complex Class II Deficiency from India.
evidence.snippet: A defect in the factors that regulate the expression of MHC class II genes resulting in the absence or reduced expression of MHC class II molecules on the immune cells is thought to be the underlying cause of MHC class II deficiency

nodes.description: Biallelic defects in CIITA or the RFX complex genes RFXANK, RFX5, and RFXAP disrupt the transcription-factor machinery responsible for MHC-II gene expression.

evidence.reference_title: Molecular genetics of the Bare lymphocyte syndrome.
evidence.snippet: The genetic lesions responsible for BLS do not lie within the MHC-II locus itself, but reside instead in genes encoding transcription factors controlling MHC-II expression.

nodes.description: Loss of CIITA or RFX-complex function impairs transcriptional activation of HLA class II genes in professional antigen-presenting cells and thymic epithelium.

evidence.reference_title: Molecular genetics of the Bare lymphocyte syndrome.
evidence.snippet: CIITA (Class II Transactivator) is a transcriptional coactivator that functions as a master control factor dictating the cell type specificity, induction and level of MHC-II expression.

evidence.reference_title: Immunodeficiency by defective expression of MHC class II (Orphanet structured-database record)
evidence.snippet: RFX5 | regulatory factor X5 | hgnc:9986 | Disease-causing germline mutation(s) in

evidence.reference_title: Molecular genetics of the Bare lymphocyte syndrome.
evidence.snippet: RFXANK, RFX5 and RFXAP are the three subunits of RFX (regulatory factor X), a DNA-binding complex that binds to a conserved cis-acting sequence, the X box, present in the promoters of all MHC-II genes.

evidence.reference_title: RFX5 / MHC class II deficiency (Definitive)
evidence.snippet: RFX5 | HGNC:9986 | MHC class II deficiency | MONDO:0008855 | AR | Definitive

evidence.reference_title: Immunodeficiency by defective expression of MHC class II (Orphanet structured-database record)
evidence.snippet: RFXANK | regulatory factor X associated ankyrin containing protein | hgnc:9987 | Disease-causing germline mutation(s) in

evidence.reference_title: Molecular genetics of the Bare lymphocyte syndrome.
evidence.snippet: RFXANK, RFX5 and RFXAP are the three subunits of RFX (regulatory factor X), a DNA-binding complex that binds to a conserved cis-acting sequence, the X box, present in the promoters of all MHC-II genes.

evidence.reference_title: RFXANK / MHC class II deficiency (Definitive)
evidence.snippet: RFXANK | HGNC:9987 | MHC class II deficiency | MONDO:0008855 | AR | Definitive

evidence.reference_title: Immunodeficiency by defective expression of MHC class II (Orphanet structured-database record)
evidence.snippet: RFXAP | regulatory factor X associated protein | hgnc:9988 | Disease-causing germline mutation(s) in

evidence.reference_title: Mutation of RFXAP, a regulator of MHC class II genes, in primary MHC class II deficiency.
evidence.snippet: Transfection with the wild-type RFXAP gene restored the expression of MHC class II molecules in the patients' cells.

evidence.reference_title: RFXAP / MHC class II deficiency (Definitive)
evidence.snippet: RFXAP | HGNC:9988 | MHC class II deficiency | MONDO:0008855 | AR | Definitive

nodes.description: Recurrent gastrointestinal infection and infectious diarrhea are common manifestations of combined immune deficiency.

evidence.reference_title: Immunodeficiency by defective expression of MHC class II (Orphanet structured-database record)
evidence.snippet: HP:0004798 | Recurrent infection of the gastrointestinal tract | Very frequent (99-80%)

nodes.description: Severe recurrent respiratory infections are very frequent and often begin in infancy.

evidence.reference_title: Immunodeficiency by defective expression of MHC class II (Orphanet structured-database record)
evidence.snippet: HP:0002205 | Recurrent respiratory infections | Very frequent (99-80%)

evidence.reference_title: Clinical, Immunological, and Molecular Findings in Five Patients with Major Histocompatibility Complex Class II Deficiency from India.
evidence.snippet: Recurrent lower respiratory tract infection was the predominant clinical manifestation among the patients.

nodes.description: Absence of MHC-II expression perturbs thymic epithelial cell maturation, central tolerance, regulatory T-cell generation or function, and peripheral B-cell tolerance, contributing to autoimmunity in a subset of patients.

evidence.reference_title: Thymic Epithelial Cell Alterations and Defective Thymopoiesis Lead to Central and Peripheral Tolerance Perturbation in MHCII Deficiency.
evidence.snippet: we demonstrated that impairment of lymphostromal cross-talk in the thymus of MHCII-/- mice affects mTEC maturation and promiscuous gene expression and causes defects of central tolerance.
